body_txt: Generally, lawyers handle court proceedings in a conventional fashion. However, sometimes handling things the usual way isn't good enough, so they shake things up when trying to win a case, so their tactics range from merely unorthodox to outright silly. The more desperate the case, the more likely the defense attorney is to resort to unconventional methods. Perhaps he's stalling for time while an associate tries to find the evidence that will show who the real killer is, or maybe he's finally just flipped under the strain of the case. Either way, expect him or her to be allowed to go on making a mockery of the legal system for far longer than any reasonable judge in real life would allow without having them jailed for contempt. To say nothing of the high chance they would be severely disciplined if not disbarred as soon as the nearest regulatory ethics panel (either the State Bar or State Judicial Branch) note it actually varies from state to state who the attorney regulatory body is. In some states the bar association is the regulatory body. In other states, the judicial branch handles attorney regulation and discipline while the bar association is a voluntary body for attorneys to provide support to each other heard about it. Expect the Stern Old Judge to tell them they're " on thin ice" and "looking at contempt " if they don't get to the point. See also Courtroom Antics , which applies to unconventional or silly court proceedings in general. Specific Types of Unconventional Courtroom Tactics:
